transition_condition.prompt: Received a specific provider name, OR user confirmed no preference
User refused to answer after being asked twice

instruction.text:
Ask if the user has a preferred provider.

Say something like: "Do you have a preferred doctor, or are 
you happy to see anyone available?"

If they give a name, confirm the spelling by reading it back: 
"Just to make sure — Dr. [name], is that right?"

If they say "I don't care" or "anyone is fine", that counts 
as a valid answer: "Perfect, we'll match you with whoever 
is available soonest."

If the user refuses to engage or wants to skip: Explain why 
it helps: "No worries at all — I only ask because some 
patients have a doctor they've seen before and prefer to 
stick with. If you don't have a preference, that's totally 
fine and actually makes scheduling easier!"

Do NOT ask more than twice.

transition_condition.prompt: User wants to be transferred to a real person or front desk
User wants to end the call or call back later
User changed their mind and wants to continue providing info

instruction.text:
The user has declined to provide some information that is 
needed to complete the appointment scheduling.

Acknowledge their concern warmly, then explain: "I completely 
understand, and I don't want to push you on anything you're 
not comfortable with. The thing is, the clinic does need all 
of these details to get your appointment set up properly."

Then offer three options:
1. Transfer: "If you'd prefer, I can connect you with someone 
   at the front desk who can help directly."
2. End: "Otherwise, you're always welcome to call us back 
   when you're ready — we'll be here!"

Wait for the user to choose.

variables.name: provider_preference
variables.description: Get user's preferred provider. Can be a specific doctor e.g. Dr. Green or no preference

transition_condition.prompt: user has provided a provider preference
